calibration_adjustment.applicable_pattern: High conviction correlates with correctness in product/founder but backfires in macro/structural
calibration_adjustment.adjustment_text: Your high-conviction macro/structural calls have a low hit rate. Cities-as-talent-magnets, super-angels-displace-VCs — both were wrong. This question is structural. Treat your Bay Area conviction as a warning signal, not a guide.

synthesized_take: Expand to global remote founders, with structure. Your pattern is clear: high conviction on macro/structural questions backfires — cities-as-talent-magnets was partially wrong, super-angels-displace-VCs was wrong. This question triggers that exact pattern. The Bay Area instinct is your weakest signal here. Net move: diversify deliberately.
